exercises.name: Acl Post Op Rehab: Balance Board
exercises.simplyPut: A gentle, therapeutic exercise designed for recovery. Acl Post Op Rehab: Balance Board carefully strengthens your acl while respecting your body's healing process. Always work within pain-free range.

mistakes.error: Doing too much too soon post-injury/surgery
mistakes.consequence: Re-injury, surgical repair failure, prolonged recovery
mistakes.correction: Follow protocol phases strictly; pain should not increase with exercise

mistakes.error: Skipping prescribed exercises (doing only ones you like)
mistakes.consequence: Imbalanced recovery; compensatory patterns persist
mistakes.correction: Complete full prescribed program; boring exercises often most important

mistakes.error: Fear-avoidance: not moving enough
mistakes.consequence: Deconditioning, chronic pain, prolonged disability
mistakes.correction: Graded exposure: small progressive steps; pain education; movement is medicine

mistakes.error: Stopping rehab once pain reduces
mistakes.consequence: Incomplete recovery; re-injury rate 2-6x higher without full rehab
mistakes.correction: Complete full rehab program even after pain resolves; functional testing before discharge

mistakes.error: Not communicating with therapist about pain
mistakes.consequence: Program not adjusted appropriately; unnecessary suffering
mistakes.correction: Use 0-10 pain scale; report honestly; pain during rehab should be <4/10

physiologyLinks.effect: Controlled loading reduces chronic inflammation via mechanotransduction
Weight-bearing rehab prevents osteopenia during recovery
Early mobilization reduces muscle loss by 50% vs bed rest
Graded exercise increases endogenous opioid release

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.simplyPut: On hands and knees, extend your right arm forward and left leg backward simultaneously, then switch. This is the gold standard rehabilitation exercise for lower back pain — it strengthens the deep stabilizer muscles of your spine without putting pressure on your discs.
exercises.howTo:
1. Start on hands and knees (tabletop position)
2. Hands under shoulders, knees under hips
3. Brace core gently (50% effort, not maximum)
4. Slowly extend right arm forward and left leg backward
5. Reach to create a straight line from fingertips to toes
6. Hold 3-5 seconds, maintaining level hips (don't rotate)
7. Return to start with control
8. Repeat on opposite side
9. Do not let your back arch or sag — it should stay flat throughout

mistakes.error: Doing too much too soon post-injury/surgery
mistakes.consequence: Re-injury, surgical repair failure, prolonged recovery
mistakes.correction: Follow protocol phases strictly; pain should not increase with exercise

mistakes.error: Skipping prescribed exercises (doing only ones you like)
mistakes.consequence: Imbalanced recovery; compensatory patterns persist
mistakes.correction: Complete full prescribed program; boring exercises often most important

mistakes.error: Fear-avoidance: not moving enough
mistakes.consequence: Deconditioning, chronic pain, prolonged disability
mistakes.correction: Graded exposure: small progressive steps; pain education; movement is medicine

mistakes.error: Stopping rehab once pain reduces
mistakes.consequence: Incomplete recovery; re-injury rate 2-6x higher without full rehab
mistakes.correction: Complete full rehab program even after pain resolves; functional testing before discharge

mistakes.error: Not communicating with therapist about pain
mistakes.consequence: Program not adjusted appropriately; unnecessary suffering
mistakes.correction: Use 0-10 pain scale; report honestly; pain during rehab should be <4/10

physiologyLinks.effect: Weight-bearing rehab prevents osteopenia during recovery
Early mobilization reduces muscle loss by 50% vs bed rest
Controlled loading reduces chronic inflammation via mechanotransduction
Graded exercise increases endogenous opioid release

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.simplyPut: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, then push your hips toward the ceiling. This gentle exercise activates your glutes (the muscles you sit on all day) and is the first step in rehabilitating lower back pain, hip problems, and knee issues.
exercises.howTo: Perform Glute Bridge slowly and within pain-free range of motion.

mistakes.correction: Follow protocol phases strictly; pain should not increase with exercise

mistakes.error: Skipping prescribed exercises (doing only ones you like)
mistakes.consequence: Imbalanced recovery; compensatory patterns persist
mistakes.correction: Complete full prescribed program; boring exercises often most important

mistakes.error: Fear-avoidance: not moving enough
mistakes.consequence: Deconditioning, chronic pain, prolonged disability
mistakes.correction: Graded exposure: small progressive steps; pain education; movement is medicine

mistakes.error: Stopping rehab once pain reduces
mistakes.consequence: Incomplete recovery; re-injury rate 2-6x higher without full rehab
mistakes.correction: Complete full rehab program even after pain resolves; functional testing before discharge

mistakes.error: Not communicating with therapist about pain
mistakes.consequence: Program not adjusted appropriately; unnecessary suffering
mistakes.correction: Use 0-10 pain scale; report honestly; pain during rehab should be <4/10

physiologyLinks.effect: Weight-bearing rehab prevents osteopenia during recovery
Controlled loading reduces chronic inflammation via mechanotransduction
Early mobilization reduces muscle loss by 50% vs bed rest
Graded exercise increases endogenous opioid release

mistakes.error: Stopping rehab once pain reduces
mistakes.consequence: Incomplete recovery; re-injury rate 2-6x higher without full rehab
mistakes.correction: Complete full rehab program even after pain resolves; functional testing before discharge

mistakes.error: Not communicating with therapist about pain
mistakes.consequence: Program not adjusted appropriately; unnecessary suffering
mistakes.correction: Use 0-10 pain scale; report honestly; pain during rehab should be <4/10

exercises.name: Wrist Roller
exercises.simplyPut: A gentle, therapeutic exercise designed for recovery. Wrist Roller carefully strengthens your forearms while respecting your body's healing process. Always work within pain-free range.

mistakes.error: Doing too much too soon post-injury/surgery
mistakes.consequence: Re-injury, surgical repair failure, prolonged recovery
mistakes.correction: Follow protocol phases strictly; pain should not increase with exercise

mistakes.error: Skipping prescribed exercises (doing only ones you like)
mistakes.consequence: Imbalanced recovery; compensatory patterns persist
mistakes.correction: Complete full prescribed program; boring exercises often most important
